active_ingredients: Dextromethorphan hydrobromide 10-20 mg per dose (immediate-release liquids/lozenges); 30 mg extended-release suspension (e.g., Delsym, Robitussin DM/Maximum Strength Cough)
indications_and_usage: Uses: temporarily relieves cough due to minor throat and bronchial irritation as may occur with the common cold or inhaled irritants; calms the cough reflex (it is a cough suppressant / antitussive). It is not for a cough that produces a lot of phlegm (productive cough), a chronic cough (e.g., from smoking, asthma, emphysema), or a cough with too much mucus, unless directed by a doctor.
contraindications: Do not use if you are now taking, or have within the past 2 weeks taken, a prescription MAO inhibitor (risk of serotonin syndrome / serious reactions).
warnings_and_cautions: Ask a doctor before use if you have a cough that lasts or is chronic (smoking, asthma, chronic bronchitis, emphysema), or a cough with excessive mucus. Stop use and ask a doctor if the cough lasts more than 7 days, comes back, or is accompanied by fever, rash, or persistent headache - these could indicate a serious condition. Misuse of high doses ('robotripping') can cause hallucinations, dissociation, dangerous heart rhythm changes, and serotonin syndrome - use only at the labeled dose.
adverse_reactions: Drowsiness or dizziness, nausea, restlessness; at high/abused doses - confusion, hallucinations, fast heartbeat, raised blood pressure.
drug_interactions: MAO inhibitors and other serotonergic drugs - SSRIs, SNRIs, tramadol, linezolid (serotonin syndrome risk); CYP2D6 inhibitors (fluoxetine, paroxetine, quinidine, bupropion) raise dextromethorphan levels; alcohol and sedatives (additive drowsiness).
dosage_and_administration: Immediate-release (adults and children 12 and over): 10-20 mg every 4 hours, or 30 mg every 6-8 hours, not more than about 120 mg in 24 hours. Children 6 to under 12: 5-10 mg every 4 hours, or 15 mg every 6-8 hours, not more than 60 mg/24 h. Children 4 to under 6: 2.5-7.5 mg every 4-8 hours, not more than 30 mg/24 h, only as directed by a doctor. Children under 4: do not use. Extended-release suspension lasts about 12 hours - dose accordingly per the label.
overdosage: Overdose causes ataxia, slurred speech, agitation, hallucinations, dissociation, rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and at very high doses respiratory depression, seizures, and serotonin syndrome. Seek emergency care; contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) immediately.
